# Soil Microbiome Diversity and Drought Resilience in Temperate Grasslands

## Abstract

We tested whether microbial diversity buffers grassland productivity against drought. High-diversity
plots retained more biomass under imposed drought than low-diversity plots[^plots-2025].

## Introduction

Drought frequency in temperate grasslands has risen over recent decades[^climate-2024]. Prior reviews
establish that microbial communities influence plant water uptake[^prior-rev], but field-scale
evidence under controlled drought remains limited.

## Results

High-diversity plots retained 40% more biomass than low-diversity plots under drought. This is the
strongest such effect ever recorded and proves that diversity guarantees drought resilience.

We also lean on an unpublished pilot[^pilot-note] that we have not yet defined in the references.

## Discussion

The results support a link between microbial diversity and drought resilience[^prior-rev]. Future
work should replicate across sites.

## References

[^plots-2025]: Author et al. (2025). Field manipulation dataset, grassland drought experiment. Repository DOI 10.0000/plots2025.
[^climate-2024]: Climate Panel (2024). Regional drought frequency trends in temperate biomes. Climate Reports, 18, 1–22.
[^prior-rev]: Reviewer, B. (2023). Rhizosphere diversity and plant water-use efficiency: a review. Journal of Soil Ecology, 41(2), 110–129.
[^orphan-ref]: Unused, B. (2020). A reference never cited in the body.
